annotators: TWO INDEPENDENT AI PASSES (Claude sessions) — honestly labeled: AI, NOT human. Different prompt framings (search-relevance rater vs answerability auditor), independently shuffled order, blinded ids, no shared notes. ~10 sample pairs are listed below and in the PR description for a human owner spot-check.

round_1.design: every (query, gold) pair, gold-only set (148 pairs), two passes
round_1.cohen_kappa: UNDEFINED — both marginals degenerate (all-yes), p_expected = 1. Reported honestly: an all-positive pair set gives ceiling agreement and proves little, which motivated round 2.

round_2.design: calibrated: 148 gold pairs + 40 seeded BAIT pairs (query x its own top-ranked BM25 hard negative; seed 333), opaque blinded pair ids, independent shuffles (seeds 4441/4442), fresh sessions, same two framings
round_2.note_on_kappa: computed over the mixed 188-pair set (both verdict categories used: 149 yes / 39 no per pass, identical verdict sets)

bait_pairs_accepted_by_both.resolution: REPAIRED by relabeling: both passes independently found this note ('the ledger runs on PostgreSQL 16 ...') a correct partial answer to 'storage engine decisions: postgresql ledger, parquet duckdb features, elasticsearch tickets'. Kept as a hard negative it would have been label noise biased against the lexical arm, so it was added to q102's gold set (the pair is already dual-pass-accepted; no further re-adjudication needed). q102 overlap/bucket/hard-negatives re-derived by the builder and re-verified by verify_semantic_v1.py.

round_2.residual_noise_estimate: 1/40 sampled top-rank hard negatives (2.5%) was an unlabeled positive. The remaining ~2,340 mined hard negatives were NOT exhaustively adjudicated; residual unlabeled positives at a low rate are a known, honestly-reported weakness of this fixture.

human_spot_check_sample.query: why did shoppers get kicked out of checkout during the november atlas outage?
human_spot_check_sample.doc_text: Atlas incident 2025-11-03: the Redis session store hit maxmemory and started evicting live checkout sessions, so users were logged out mid-payment. Fix: dedicated Redis instance with noeviction and alerts at 80% memory.

human_spot_check_sample.query: a customer's money-back attempt failed — what's the right way to try it again?
human_spot_check_sample.doc_text: Atlas how-to: to reissue a failed refund, requeue the refund intent from the dead-letter topic with the original charge id; never create a new refund object by hand.

human_spot_check_sample.query: are we allowed to send promo messages at night?
human_spot_check_sample.doc_text: Beacon constraint: marketing pushes must respect user-local quiet hours from 22:00 to 08:00; transactional notifications such as delivery updates are exempt.

human_spot_check_sample.query: how stale can the fraud model's inputs get before somebody is alerted?
human_spot_check_sample.doc_text: Cinder constraint: features used by the fraud model must be fresher than 15 minutes; the pipeline emits a freshness metric per feature and alerts when the SLA is breached.

human_spot_check_sample.query: where do machine learning jobs run in the cluster, and can anything else land on those machines?
human_spot_check_sample.doc_text: Drift constraint: GPU node pools autoscale from zero and carry taints so only ML workloads schedule there; everything else runs on the general spot pool with a small on-demand floor.

human_spot_check_sample.query: search doesn't find tickets that say 'bill' when someone types 'invoice' — how do we fix that?
human_spot_check_sample.doc_text: Ember how-to: to improve ticket search, add domain synonyms to the Elasticsearch synonyms file (for example 'invoice' matching 'bill'); reindexing is not needed because synonyms apply at query time.

human_spot_check_sample.query: which past outages were traced back to our caching layer?
human_spot_check_sample.doc_text: Beacon incident 2025-08-27: a Redis cluster failover dropped the push-notification dedupe set, so users received duplicate pushes for two hours. Fix: persist dedupe keys with AOF and a replay guard.

human_spot_check_sample.query: storage engine decisions: postgresql ledger, parquet duckdb features, elasticsearch tickets
human_spot_check_sample.doc_text: Atlas note: the ledger runs on PostgreSQL 16 with synchronous replication to a standby in a second availability zone; failover is manual by design.

human_spot_check_sample.query: what numeric type do we use for storing prices?
human_spot_check_sample.doc_text: Atlas convention: all monetary amounts are stored as integer minor units (cents) with an ISO 4217 currency code; floating point money is banned in code review.

human_spot_check_sample.query: the latest deploy broke everything — how do i get back to the previous version?
human_spot_check_sample.doc_text: Drift how-to: to roll back a bad release, revert the release-branch commit and let ArgoCD converge; never delete pods by hand, the controller will fight you.
